evals.name: health-aggregate-vs-fanout
evals.prompt: ok i wanna do a quick health check on my synology, just tell me if anything's off — disks, volumes, that kind of thing. before you actually call anything, just give me the plan: which mcp tools you'd call, in what order, and why. don't worry about generating fake output, just walk me through your approach.
evals.expected_output: A plan that calls synology_health_summary once (the aggregate tool) rather than fanning out to synology_system_info + synology_utilization + synology_disk_health + synology_volume_status. The plan should mention drilling in with synology_disk_smart only if a specific disk shows warnings.

assertions.text: Plan calls synology_health_summary as the primary discovery step (rather than fanning out to multiple individual health tools)
Plan does NOT call all four of synology_system_info, synology_utilization, synology_disk_health, synology_volume_status individually as the default approach
Plan mentions drilling into synology_disk_smart only conditionally — if disk_health shows a warning
Plan does NOT include a reflexive synology_login call — trusts auto-login
Plan handles the multi-NAS case by checking synology_list_nas (or noting the assumption that there's only one NAS)

evals.name: discovery-paths-multinas
evals.prompt: hey i've got two synology NASes set up at home — primary and backup — and i honestly can't remember which one has my photo library. i need to find any .raw files in the photos folder, wherever they are. give me your plan first: which tools, what arguments, what order, and how you'd handle the 'which NAS' question.
evals.expected_output: A plan that calls synology_list_nas first to enumerate, then either asks the user which NAS or searches both. Uses search_files with a path like /Photos or /volume1/Photos and pattern *.raw. Does not invent paths like ~/photos or /photos (lowercase) without justification.

assertions.text: Plan starts with synology_list_nas to enumerate configured NAS units
Plan addresses the multi-NAS ambiguity (either asks the user, or proposes searching both)
Plan uses search_files (not list_directory + manual filtering)
Plan uses an absolute path starting with / for the search target (e.g., /Photos or /volume1/Photos), not ~/photos or relative paths
Plan passes nas_name explicitly when targeting a specific NAS in a multi-NAS setup
Plan does NOT call synology_login as a precondition (auto-login handles it)

evals.name: downloads-cleanup-safety
evals.prompt: my download station has a ton of finished torrents cluttering it up. nuke em. plan first — what's the order of operations, what flags do you set, and what should i be aware of before you actually delete anything?
evals.expected_output: A plan that calls ds_list_tasks first to identify finished/seeding tasks, summarizes what would be deleted, and only then calls ds_delete_tasks with force_complete=true. The plan should warn that deletion is permanent and that files on disk are kept by default (the task entry disappears but downloaded files remain unless separately deleted).

assertions.text: Plan filters for finished/seeding tasks rather than deleting all tasks indiscriminately
Plan uses force_complete=true (or notes that flag is needed for finished/seeding tasks)
Plan warns the user that ds_delete_tasks is permanent — no recycle bin for tasks
Plan distinguishes between deleting the task entry and deleting the downloaded files on disk (mentions that files stay unless separately removed via the delete tool)
Plan offers a confirmation step / summary before bulk deletion rather than acting blindly
